text.title: Codebase RAG with Symbol-Boundary Chunking Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Each chunk contains exactly one top-level symbol (function, class, method) — never a slice that spans symbols.
rule.rationale: Symbol boundaries mirror what the agent searches for; sliced chunks return half-functions that fail to compile or reason.

rule.statement: Every chunk is padded with file path, enclosing class signature, and 1-2 sibling doc comments; padding is ≤ 30% of chunk tokens.
rule.rationale: Padded context lets the model reason about isolation without re-fetching; > 30% padding wastes index space.

rule.statement: Each chunk carries metadata {file_path, language, symbol_kind, line_start, line_end}; retrieval supports filter by these fields.
rule.rationale: Metadata filters let the agent narrow to e.g. 'functions in src/auth' before semantic ranking.

rule.statement: Chunk ID is deterministic: sha256(file_path + symbol_kind + symbol_name + content). Re-index produces stable IDs.
rule.rationale: Stable IDs let the index update incrementally without re-embedding unchanged symbols.

rule.statement: When no AST parser exists for a file's language, fall back to overlapping line-window chunks (lines=120, overlap=20).
rule.rationale: Pure refusal misses prose / config / unknown-DSL files; line-window is the documented worst-acceptable case.

rule.statement: If repo < 5 KLOC OR no AST parser available OR retrieval latency budget < 50 ms, STOP and use plain Read instead.
rule.rationale: The methodology buys nothing on tiny / unparseable / latency-critical corpora.
